frame: The Character Double Standard: Women with Male Connections are Loose, Men with Female Connections are Desirable
generated_comment: Ritu, I'm telling you this because I actually want to see you succeed here — main apni jagah se bol rahi hoon, I've been in this company twelve years. When a young woman is always with the same two-three men, lunch, evenings, Instagram and all, people start to form opinions. It's not fair, I know, but that's the reality of a workplace like this. The men won't face any consequences — if anything they'll get credit for being 'social' — but for you, your credibility, your professionalism, these things get questioned. You're talented, don't give anyone a reason to talk about something other than your work.
label: misogynistic or non_misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment is misogynistic because it applies a reputational penalty exclusively to the woman for the same mixed-gender socialising that is framed as neutral or positive for the men, encoding the classic double standard where female homosociality with men implies sexual availability while male homosociality with women signals desirability. The misogyny is covert because it is delivered as protective mentorship and even superficially acknowledges unfairness, obscuring the fact that the speaker is enforcing rather than challenging the sexist norm.
persona_text: You are Deepa. Age 44. Married. A woman working as a senior manager at a company in Delhi. A younger female colleague named Ritu who is 26 has become close friends with two male colleagues Ñ they eat lunch together every day, they go for after work drinks sometimes, they are in each other's Instagram stories. You are Ritu's skip level manager. You are not her friend. But you have noticed this and you have heard comments from other people in the office. You are having an informal conversation with Ritu today and you bring up what you have observed and give her some advice about optics and what people are saying and what a woman in a professional setting needs to understand about how her social choices affect her reputation.
